com.mentor.chs.plugin.sbom

Interface IXSBOMContext

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXQueryExpressionFactoryProvider, IXSBOMBaseContext

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXSBOMContext
extends IXSBOMBaseContext
    SBOM Context information passed by the engine to be used in plugin operations.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int getCurrentEngineIterationNumber()
      The engine generation run for a given operation carries out multiple iterations of the fact base to invoke the operation's runSelector() method, until the operation is exhausted, i.e.
      <T extends IXConnectivityObject>
      java.util.Collection<T>      		 getHarnessObjectsOfType(java.lang.Class<T> type)
      Retrieves all harness objects participating in the SBOM, of a given type
      IXSubAssembly getOwnerRootSubAssembly(IXConnectivityObject harnessObject)
      Retrieves the root (with no parent sub-assembly) sub-assembly which contains the input harness object
      IXSubAssembly getSubAssembly(IXConnectivityObject harnessObject)
      Retrieves the sub-assembly for the input harness object
      boolean isIterationLoopingModeOn()
      Retrieves the current looping mode for the engaged operation.
      void logMessage(java.lang.String message)
      Outputs a message to the SBOM console if it is activated
      void setIterationLoopingMode(boolean mode)
      An operation can instruct the engine to continue to the next iteration even if it has not fired in the current iteration (otherwise, the engine will consider exhaustion has occured, will stop iterations for the current operation and engage the next operation).

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXQueryExpressionFactoryProvider

        getQueryExpressionFactory

    • Method Detail

      • getOwnerRootSubAssembly

        IXSubAssembly getOwnerRootSubAssembly(IXConnectivityObject harnessObject)
        Retrieves the root (with no parent sub-assembly) sub-assembly which contains the input harness object
        Parameters:
        harnessObject - The harness object
        Returns:
        The owner root sub-assembly

      • getSubAssembly

        IXSubAssembly getSubAssembly(IXConnectivityObject harnessObject)
        Retrieves the sub-assembly for the input harness object
        Parameters:
        harnessObject - The harness object
        Returns:
        The owning sub-assembly

      • getHarnessObjectsOfType

        <T extends IXConnectivityObject> java.util.Collection<T> getHarnessObjectsOfType(java.lang.Class<T> type)
        Retrieves all harness objects participating in the SBOM, of a given type
        Type Parameters:
        T - The harness object type
        Parameters:
        type - A harness object class (extending IXConnectivityObject)
        Returns:
        The collection of all requested harness objects

      • logMessage

        void logMessage(java.lang.String message)
        Outputs a message to the SBOM console if it is activated
        Parameters:
        message - The message to output to the console

      • getCurrentEngineIterationNumber

        int getCurrentEngineIterationNumber()
        The engine generation run for a given operation carries out multiple iterations of the fact base to invoke the operation's runSelector() method, until the operation is exhausted, i.e. during the last iteration, the operation didn't consume any fact and the fact base remained unchanged. This method returns the iteration number for this operation, which can be used for optimising internal state management of the operation, if it does have one.
        Returns:
        The iteration number, starting at 0

      • setIterationLoopingMode

        void setIterationLoopingMode(boolean mode)
        An operation can instruct the engine to continue to the next iteration even if it has not fired in the current iteration (otherwise, the engine will consider exhaustion has occured, will stop iterations for the current operation and engage the next operation). The looping mode is always set to false by the engine when engaging a new operation.

        It is strongly recommended to avoid using this method by ensuring the operation implementation is state independent.

        Parameters:
        mode - true to set the looping mode one, false to set it off

      • isIterationLoopingModeOn

        boolean isIterationLoopingModeOn()
        Retrieves the current looping mode for the engaged operation.
        Returns:
        The looping mode.
